claim: Claude Code is moving at 10x the speed of competitors due to its integrated harness and agent orchestration capabilities, enabling non-programmers to build sophisticated systems
actionable: Adopt Claude Code with million-context mode via API for maximum productivity gains when building data pipelines, data science tools, or automation systems
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: The competitiveness of AI coding agents depends more on harness quality, infrastructure, and prompt engineering than model weights alone
actionable: Build model-specific harnesses with tailored prompts rather than assuming a single prompt works across Claude, GPT, and Kimi models, as they respond differently
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: SaaS companies face structural collapse as the application layer consolidates into agents, file systems, and CRUD databases, with value flowing to scalable data/compute infrastructure
actionable: If building SaaS, focus on scalable data platforms (Databricks, Snowflake) rather than point solutions that agents can replace with vibe-coded alternatives
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: Claude Code skills that encode domain expertise and knowledge (e.g., earnings transcript analysis, data center modeling) become a competitive moat beyond model weights alone
actionable: Build and reuse domain-specific skills in Claude Code by encoding expert knowledge in prompts and KB cache blocks that can be shared across team members
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: The junior developer job market is being eliminated in real-time by Claude Code and Cursor, but higher-level roles (research, design, strategy) are growing faster
actionable: Career advice: pivot toward domain expertise (data science, research, product strategy) that agents cannot currently do well rather than pure coding skills
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: Cost-per-token is declining by ~1000x year-over-year through combined improvements in hardware, software, RL training, and model architecture, not just scaling
actionable: Budget for 10-100x reductions in inference costs annually when planning long-term AI infrastructure; assume yesterday's assumptions are outdated
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: Open-source models (DeepSeek V4, Kimi) are being trained on smuggled or rented Nvidia Blackwell chips in Southeast Asia, then weights extracted and distributed via encrypted FTP
actionable: Assume Chinese AI labs have partial access to latest Nvidia hardware; use technical analysis of model capabilities rather than supply chain assumptions to evaluate competitive threats
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: Prompt engineering varies dramatically by model: Opus prefers calm instructions (no caps, no bolding), while GPT-5.2 requires aggressive framing (all caps, urgency, conflict)
actionable: When building daisy-chain prompts across multiple models, reverse your prompt style for each model based on observed responsiveness rather than using uniform templates
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: Anthropic's refusal to deploy models in government weapons systems or mass surveillance is dogmatic and strategically naive, enabling OpenAI to capture DoD contracts by accepting weaker restrictions
actionable: For AI labs facing government pressure: frame compliance as pragmatic risk reduction rather than ethical purity; governments will extract capabilities regardless, so better to guide the process
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: Scaling laws for both pre-training and RL continue unbroken; paradigm shifts occur every 3-6 months with new model releases, not years, so compute spending should continue accelerating
actionable: Trust continued scaling law breakthroughs as justification for massive capex by Anthropic/OpenAI; don't assume Satya Nadella's 'algorithmic innovation breaks the math' argument
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: The US military using Claude 3.5 Sonnet (an old model) in classified networks is strategically dangerous because China uses cutting-edge models (Qwen, Kimi, DeepSeek) in military applications
actionable: Government procurement processes move too slowly; advocate for continuous model updates rather than locked versions or expect capability gaps to widen faster than technical advantages accrue
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: Hyperscaler capex on data centers (Amazon $200B, Google $180B) is rational, not excessive, because pre-training and RL scaling laws show no end in sight with 10-100x capability improvements per $B spent
actionable: Capacity constraints are real and worsening; if you need inference at scale, secure commitments early or expect price spikes and availability limits
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: Memory and wafer shortage is causing a reallocation: AI gets data center GPUs/memory while consumer devices (phones, PCs) and gaming GPUs are starved, locking out hobbyist inference
actionable: Don't invest in local inference clusters or gaming GPUs for production use; costs will rise 2-5x as memory prices spike and supply is rationed to data centers
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: UBI (Universal Basic Income) is becoming economically necessary because AI dislocation will eliminate white-collar jobs faster than the economy can retrain 2-3 million workers
actionable: Plan hiring and workforce strategy with assumption that middle-office and junior roles collapse in 1-3 years; focus on reskilling or shrinking team size rather than growth
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: Social media's amplification of wealth perception and income inequality (not absolute living standards) is driving political backlash against AI; Democrats will win next election by becoming anti-AI
actionable: Expect anti-AI regulation and political scrutiny to intensify as job losses accelerate; prepare corporate messaging around 'responsible AI' or workforce transition programs
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: A 'buffet of dopamine' (infinite low-effort content) produces less happiness than sustained, difficult projects; people's perception of decline despite objective living standard improvements is driven by comparison, not reality
actionable: Structure work and personal projects around deep engagement and incremental difficulty rather than optimizing for immediate gratification; long-term satisfaction scales differently
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: Chinese AI models are distilling from Anthropic and OpenAI APIs via proxy traffic through Cursor, Lovable, and Replit coding platforms, not direct API access
actionable: Reduce API logging to third-party coding platforms if protecting model outputs; assume any high-traffic proxy tool is a vector for distillation
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: Anthropic's models are better than OpenAI's for the same compute budget, suggesting better training methodology/data pipeline rather than just scale differences
actionable: If comparing models by price/capability ratio rather than absolute capability, prioritize Anthropic models; they extract more performance per dollar of compute spent
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence

claim: Microsoft's refusal to massively increase data center capex and pullback on OpenAI exclusivity is a strategic mistake that will cost them the AI era, surrendering compute share to Amazon and Google
actionable: As a builder, diversify compute sourcing away from Microsoft; they are likely to lag in availability and innovation as Amazon/Google capture leading-edge capacity
source_title: Dylan Patel: AI in War, Jobs are Cooked, Chinese Hacking, Microsoft Cope, and Super Intelligence
